Notice Of Motion And Motion To Apportion Proceeding And For Order To Distribute Settlement Funds
Off Calendar May-19-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Amir Sarreshtehdary of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Elvira Tinoco et al.
Parties appearing in the Courtroom were: Plaintiffs Elvira Tinoco, Catalina Mosqueda, Martin Mosqueda, Patricia Mosqueda-Galang, Raquel Mosqueda, and Michael Nieve. Anthea Mosqueda and Veronica Mosqueda made claims as successors-in-interest to the wrongful death claims Alfredo Mosqueda, Jr. who was a statutory wrongful death heir, deceased.

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS TINOCO, ELVIRA AS WRONGFUL DEATH HEIR AND AS SUCCESSOR-IN-INTEREST TO ALFREDO MOSQUEDA, DECEASED, ET AL.'S MOTION TO APPORTION PROCEEDING AND FOR ORDER TO DISTRIBUTE SETTLEMENT FUNDS

Hearing held. The Court made the following ruling: Plaintiffs' motion for apportionment proceeding and for order to distribute settlement funds is granted. The plaintiffs are entitled to distribution of all net funds in the following proportions: Estate of Alfredo Mosqueda, Sr. - 12.5%, Estate of Alfredo Mosqueda, Jr. - 12.5%, Elvira Tinoco - 12.5%, Martin Mosqueda - 12.5%, Patricia Mosqueda - 12.5%, Raquel Mosqueda - 12.5%, Catalina Mosqueda - 12.5%, Michael Nieve - 12.5%. The Court further ordered that the signature of Elvira Tinoco shall be sufficient on all settlement documents for the purpose of settling survival/wrongful claims. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Kimberly R. Hendershott of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12552 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL DATE AND RE-OPEN EXPERT DISCOVERY

No objections made and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion to continue the trial date and re-open expert discovery for the limited purpose of replacing plaintiffs' expert Dr. Herman Bruch is granted. The trial date is continued to August 10, 2015 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. Expert discovery is re-opened solely to allow the following: plaintiffs may designate an expert in place of Dr. Bruch and such expert may prepare a report and be presented for deposition; if needed, defendants may supplement their expert witness designation limited to responding to plaintiffs' new designation.
